description_long: This object consists of two tall, irregularly shaped stone slabs standing closely side by side, separated by a narrow vertical gap. The stones have a rugged, natural appearance with uneven surfaces, jagged edges, and a variety of subtle gray tones. The texture is coarse, with visible striations and weathered patches that suggest long-term exposure to the elements. The stones are vertically oriented, with one slab slightly taller and more slender than the other, creating an asymmetrical silhouette. The coloration ranges from dark charcoal to lighter gray, with occasional hints of bluish undertones. The overall impression is of a substantial, ancient stone barrier that could serve as a natural obstacle or boundary in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A pair of tall, rugged stone slabs with jagged edges and coarse, weathered gray surfaces, separated by a narrow vertical gap. The stones have an asymmetrical, natural look and substantial presence.
description_view_0: Front view shows both stone slabs side by side, highlighting the vertical gap and the irregular, jagged edges.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thin profile of the stones and emphasizes their height and narrowness.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the stones' rough, uneven backs and the continuation of the vertical gap.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the thinness and the subtle surface texture variations.
attribute_location: urban park boundary or landscaped plaza
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier, upright on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular natural striations and mottling, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible surface erosion and minor discoloration from exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban elements but can blend with natural landscaping or other stone features.
